documents.title: 5G Core Service Exposure Specification
documents.summary: Defines service exposure functions and related interfaces for 5G core capabilities.

documents.title: Network Slice Management Profile
documents.summary: Describes orchestration and lifecycle control for network slices.

documents.title: Subscriber Data Interface Guidelines
documents.summary: Specifies data exchange expectations for subscriber profile access.

dependencies.rationale: Service exposure functions require subscriber data access for request fulfillment.
Slice lifecycle orchestration depends on exposed core capabilities for control-plane coordination.
Provisioning workflows appear to require synchronized subscriber data updates.

slides.title: Executive Summary
slides.purpose: Provide a high-level overview of the dependency landscape.
content: Three telecom specifications were analyzed across core-network, orchestration, and data-management domains.
All detected dependencies cross domain boundaries, indicating strong coordination requirements.

slides.title: High-Criticality Dependencies
slides.purpose: Highlight relationships most likely to affect delivery or interoperability.
content: 5G Core Service Exposure Specification depends explicitly on Subscriber Data Interface Guidelines.
Network Slice Management Profile appears to rely on 5G Core Service Exposure capabilities for lifecycle coordination.

slides.title: Recommendations
slides.purpose: Summarize next actions for analysts and architects.
content: Validate inferred orchestration-to-core dependency with domain SMEs.
Prioritize integration testing around subscriber data access assumptions.
Track cross-domain dependency changes during standards version updates.

recommendations: Review explicit evidence for all high-criticality dependencies.
Confirm inferred dependencies through architecture workshops.
Establish version compatibility tracking between related specifications.
